question_structure.context: Two right-angled triangular blocks have base angles of $53 ^{\circ}$. Block A is fixed to the ground, and block B, with a mass of $\mathrm{m}{=}22.4\mathrm{kg}$, has a coefficient of kinetic friction with the ground of $\upmu{=}0.4$. There is a light container with a smooth, curved contact surface between the two blocks. Water is now slowly poured into the container (assuming the maximum static friction is equal to the kinetic friction, $\cos53^{\circ}~{=}0.6$, sin53° $=\!0.8$, and the acceleration due to gravity $\mathbf{g}\!=\!10\mathbf{m}/\mathbf{s}^{2}$).
question_structure.sub_question_1: When the mass of the water in the container is $\mathrm{m}_{1}\mathrm{=}2.4\mathrm{kg}$, block B remains at rest. Determine the magnitude of the normal forces exerted by the container on the two blocks at this instance.
question_structure.sub_question_2: As more water is gradually poured in, determine the magnitude of the normal forces exerted by the container on the two blocks at the instant when block B just begins to slide.

sub_question_1.step_1: Perform a force analysis on the container and the water inside, with a mass of $m_{1} = 2.4 \mathrm{kg}$, as a whole system.
sub_question_1.step_2: According to the equilibrium condition, we have: $2F\cos\theta = m_{1}g$.
sub_question_1.step_4: Based on Newton's third law, the normal force exerted by the container on the two blocks is: $F' = F = 20 \mathrm{N}$.

sub_question_2.step_5: Let $F_{1}$ be the magnitude of the normal force exerted by the container on the two blocks when block B is just about to slide. According to the equilibrium condition in the horizontal direction, we have: $F_{1}\sin\theta = f_{m}$.
sub_question_2.step_6: According to the equilibrium condition in the vertical direction, we have: $mg + F_{1}\cos\theta = F_{N}$.
sub_question_2.step_7: Based on the formula for calculating frictional force, we have: $f_{m} = \mu F_{N}$.
sub_question_2.step_8: Solving for the magnitude of the normal force exerted by the container on the two blocks when block B just starts to slide, we get $F_{1} = 160 \mathrm{N}$.

image_captions: A line drawing shows a liquid drop resting on a flat surface. Lines tangent to the edge of the drop make equal angles (θ) with the surface on both sides, labeled A and B.

result_quantity.name: Magnitude of the force exerted by each side of container on the blocks
Magnitude of normal force exerted by container on block
Magnitude of the normal force exerted by the container on the blocks when block B just starts to slide
